title: Updating "Actual Hours" via an external file to give a "Remaining Hours" figure.
excerpt:

We are wanting to add more data into our boards around hour tracking.

Actual Hours - Hours spent per milestone so far 
…

posts.author: bsim (Bevan   )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

We are wanting to add more data into our boards around hour tracking.

Currently the test board structures look like this:

Target Hours - Set per milestone
Actual Hours - Hours spent per milestone so far
Remaining Hours - Formula to remove Actual from Target

Our main system for accounting and hour tracking can export into a txt, csv, or xls file which has e.g.;

Column 1:
Milestone
Row 1 - L28
Row 2 - L29

Column 2:
Actual Hours
Row 1 - 6
Row 2 - 4

I am wanting to be able to import one of these report files and use it to overwrite the actual hours (if this can be automated that is great but we don’t mind manually importing once a day).

What is the best way to do this?

Ideally it would look like “If milestone L28 is in board, replace with Actual Hours”.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about trying a Monday. com API script designed to help you update the “Actual Hours” column in your board using data from a CSV, TXT, or XLS file. It works by:

  1. Reading milestone names and actual hours from your file.

  2. Matching those milestones to items in your Monday com board.

  3. Updating the “Actual Hours” column for each matched item.

What You’ll Need to Customize:

  • Your Monday. com API token

  • Your Board ID

  • The Column ID for “Actual Hours”

  • A file like milestone_report.csv with columns: Milestone, Actual Hours

Script Summary

# Reads milestone data from a file

# Matches each milestone to an item in your board

# Updates the ‘Actual Hours’ column using Monday. com GraphQL API

If you prefer no-code/low-code solutions you could look at:

  • Make (formerly Integromat): Great for automating CSV imports to Monday.com

  • Zapier: Can trigger updates from file uploads or Google Sheets

  • n8n: Open-source alternative for custom workflows

There are lots of pros and partners too that could quickly help you set something uop if needed. Hope this helps!
